18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
19	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
20	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
21	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
22	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
23		Log          io.Writer
24		EventSink    io.Writer
25		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
26		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
27		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, is invoked between nextCandidate and
28		// Claim. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute against the
29		// loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed routing
30		// error the upstream surfaced — notably agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible
31		// when the harness allow-list rejects the model. Any non-nil error
32		// causes the loop to exit immediately without claiming the bead, with
33		// a worker-level execution_failed record naming the rejected pair.
34		// DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list; this gate only
35		// consumes the typed-incompatibility surface.
36		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
37		Once           bool
38		PollInterval   time.Duration
39		NoReview       bool
40		LabelFilter    string
41		SessionID      string
42		WorkerID       string
43		ProjectRoot    string
44	}
45	
46	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
47	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
48	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
49	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
50	
51	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
52	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
53	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
54	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
55	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
56	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
57	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
58	
59	// capLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
60	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
61	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
62	func capLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
63		if d <= 0 {
64			return 0
65		}
66		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
67			return MaxLoopCooldown
68		}
69		return d
70	}

72	type ExecuteBeadReport struct {
73		BeadID      string `json:"bead_id"`
74		AttemptID   string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
75		WorkerID    string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
76		Harness     string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
77		Provider    string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
78		Model       string `json:"model,omitempty"`
79		Status      string `json:"status"`
80		Detail      string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
81		SessionID   string `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
82		BaseRev     string `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
83		ResultRev   string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
84		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
85		RetryAfter  string `json:"retry_after,omitempty"`
86		// NoChangesRationale carries the agent's explanation when status == no_changes.
87		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
88		// ReviewVerdict is the post-merge review verdict (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES,
89		// or BLOCK) when a reviewer ran. Empty when review was skipped.
90		ReviewVerdict string `json:"review_verdict,omitempty"`
91		// ReviewRationale carries the actionable reviewer-authored findings for
92		// non-APPROVE review outcomes.

51	// TestRoutingPreflightRejectionExitsLoopWithoutClaim covers ddx-98e6e9ef:
52	// when RoutePreflight returns the upstream typed-incompatibility error
53	// (agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible), the loop must NOT claim the bead,
54	// must NOT invoke the executor, must record a worker-level execution_failed
55	// result naming the rejected (harness, model) pair, and must exit.
56	func TestRoutingPreflightRejectionExitsLoopWithoutClaim(t *testing.T) {
57		inner, candidate, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
58		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
59	
60		var execCount int32
61		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
62			Store: store,
63			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
64				atomic.AddInt32(&execCount, 1)
65				return ExecuteBeadReport{BeadID: beadID, Status: ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess}, nil
66			}),
67		}
68	
69		// Fabricate a typed-incompatibility error via the upstream test seam:
70		// the preflight callback returns the same error type that upstream
71		// ResolveRoute returns when a harness allow-list rejects the model.
72		rejected := agentlib.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible{
73			Harness:         "claude",
74			Model:           "gpt-5",
75			SupportedModels: []string{"claude-opus-4-7", "claude-sonnet-4-6"},
76		}
77		var preflightCalls int32
78		preflight := func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error {
79			atomic.AddInt32(&preflightCalls, 1)
80			return rejected
81		}
82	
83		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{
84			Assignee: "worker",
85			Harness:  "claude",
86			Model:    "gpt-5",
87		}
88		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
89	
90		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
91			Once:           true,
92			RoutePreflight: preflight,
93		})
94		require.NoError(t, err)
95		require.NotNil(t, result)
96	
97		// AC#1: preflight ran before Claim.
98		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&preflightCalls), "preflight must be invoked")
99	
100		// AC#2: Claim was NEVER called on the store.
101		assert.Equal(t, int32(0), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls),
102			"beadStore.Claim must not be called when preflight rejects")
103	
104		// Executor must not have been invoked either.
105		assert.Equal(t, int32(0), atomic.LoadInt32(&execCount),
106			"executor must not run when preflight rejects")
107	
108		// AC#3: worker-level execution_failed record names the rejected pair.
109		require.Len(t, result.Results, 1, "exactly one worker-level failure record")
110		report := result.Results[0]
111		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, report.Status)
112		assert.Equal(t, candidate.ID, report.BeadID)
113		assert.Equal(t, "claude", report.Harness)
114		assert.Equal(t, "gpt-5", report.Model)
115		assert.Contains(t, report.Detail, "claude", "detail must name rejected harness")
116		assert.Contains(t, report.Detail, "gpt-5", "detail must name rejected model")
117		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, result.LastFailureStatus)
118		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Failures)
119	
120		// AC#4: bead has no kind:tier-attempt event from this attempt.
121		events, err := store.Events(candidate.ID)
122		require.NoError(t, err)
123		for _, ev := range events {
124			assert.NotEqual(t, "tier-attempt", ev.Kind,
125				"no tier-attempt event must be recorded when preflight rejects pre-claim")
126		}
127	
128		// Bead must remain open with no owner — confirms no Claim side-effects.
129		got, err := store.Get(candidate.ID)
130		require.NoError(t, err)
131		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status)
132		assert.Empty(t, got.Owner)
133	}

381		// DefaultWallClockMS to guarantee termination.

393	// ResolveLogDir returns an absolute session-log directory path anchored at
394	// projectRoot. Callers that construct an agent.Runner must use this to avoid
395	// the Runner's relative DefaultLogDir resolving against process CWD — which
396	// historically wrote logs to stray locations like cli/internal/server/.ddx/.
397	func ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, configured string) string {
398		if configured == "" {
399			configured = DefaultLogDir
400		}

403		}
404		if projectRoot == "" {
405			return configured
406		}
407		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, configured)

28	// Resolve produces a sealed ResolvedConfig by layering overrides onto
29	// cfg. Overrides are applied as last-write-wins per field. The
30	// returned ResolvedConfig does not alias cfg's storage — every map
31	// and slice is deep-cloned via per-type Clone methods before being
32	// captured.
33	//
34	// Resolve accepts a nil cfg and returns a ResolvedConfig populated
35	// from package defaults, sealed normally.
36	func (c *NewConfig) Resolve(overrides CLIOverrides) ResolvedConfig {
37		r := ResolvedConfig{sealed: true}
38	
39		r.assignee = overrides.Assignee
40		r.reviewMaxRetries = c.ResolveReviewMaxRetries()
41	
42		var agent *AgentConfig
43		var workers *WorkersConfig
44		if c != nil {
45			agent = c.Agent.Clone()
46			workers = c.Workers
47		}
48	
49		r.noProgressCooldown = workers.ResolveNoProgressCooldown()
50		r.maxNoChangesBeforeClose = workers.ResolveMaxNoChangesBeforeClose()
51		r.heartbeatInterval = workers.ResolveHeartbeatInterval()
52	
53		r.harness = overrides.Harness
54		if r.harness == "" && agent != nil {
55			r.harness = agent.Harness
56		}
57	
58		r.model = overrides.Model
59		if r.model == "" && agent != nil {
60			r.model = agent.Model
61		}
62	
63		r.provider = overrides.Provider
64		r.modelRef = overrides.ModelRef
65		r.profile = overrides.Profile
66		r.minTier = overrides.MinTier
67		r.maxTier = overrides.MaxTier
68		r.effort = overrides.Effort
69	
70		r.permissions = overrides.Permissions
71		if r.permissions == "" && agent != nil {
72			r.permissions = agent.Permissions
73		}
74	
75		if overrides.Timeout != nil {
76			r.timeout = *overrides.Timeout
77		} else if agent != nil && agent.TimeoutMS > 0 {
78			r.timeout = time.Duration(agent.TimeoutMS) * time.Millisecond
79		}
80	
81		r.evidenceCaps = c.ResolveEvidenceCaps(r.harness)
82	
83		r.contextBudget = overrides.ContextBudget
84		if r.contextBudget == "" && c != nil {
85			r.contextBudget = c.EvidenceCaps.ResolveContextBudget()
86		}
87	
88		if agent != nil {
89			r.sessionLogDir = agent.SessionLogDir
90			if agent.ReasoningLevels != nil {
91				r.reasoningLevels = make(map[string][]string, len(agent.ReasoningLevels))
92				for k, v := range agent.ReasoningLevels {
93					r.reasoningLevels[k] = append([]string(nil), v...)
94				}
95			}
96			if agent.Routing != nil {
97				r.resolvedLadder = map[string][]string{
98					r.profile: agent.Routing.ResolvedLadder(r.profile),
99				}
100			}
101		}
102		if r.resolvedLadder == nil {
103			r.resolvedLadder = map[string][]string{
104				r.profile: (*RoutingConfig)(nil).ResolvedLadder(r.profile),
105			}
106		}
107	
108		if c != nil && c.Executions != nil && c.Executions.Mirror != nil {
109			r.mirrorConfig = c.Executions.Mirror.Clone()
110		}
111	
112		return r

115	// LoadAndResolve is the canonical production entry point that produces
116	// a sealed ResolvedConfig. It loads the project's .ddx/config.yaml from
117	// projectRoot, layers overrides on top, and returns the immutable
118	// ResolvedConfig.
119	//
120	// On load error, LoadAndResolve still returns a usable, sealed

122	// overrides, alongside the underlying error. Callers decide whether to
123	// surface the error or proceed with the defaults-backed config.
124	//

126	func LoadAndResolve(projectRoot string, overrides CLIOverrides) (ResolvedConfig, error) {

261	// DefaultProfileLadders returns the built-in profile escalation policy.
262	func DefaultProfileLadders() map[string][]string {
263		out := make(map[string][]string, len(defaultProfileLadders))
264		for profile, ladder := range defaultProfileLadders {
265			out[profile] = append([]string(nil), ladder...)
266		}
267		return out
268	}

270	// ResolvedLadder returns the escalation ladder for the named profile. If
271	// ProfileLadders contains an entry for profile, that wins. Otherwise falls
272	// back to the deprecated ProfilePriority for the default profile only. If
273	// neither is set, returns the shipped FEAT-006 profile ladder.
274	func (r *RoutingConfig) ResolvedLadder(profile string) []string {
275		if profile == "" {
276			profile = "default"
277		}
278		if r == nil {
279			if ladder, ok := defaultProfileLadders[profile]; ok {
280				return append([]string(nil), ladder...)
281			}
282			return append([]string(nil), defaultProfileLadders["default"]...)
283		}
284		if r.ProfileLadders != nil {
285			if ladder, ok := r.ProfileLadders[profile]; ok && len(ladder) > 0 {
286				return append([]string(nil), ladder...)
287			}
288		}
289		if profile == "default" && len(r.ProfilePriority) > 0 {
290			return append([]string(nil), r.ProfilePriority...)
291		}
292		if ladder, ok := defaultProfileLadders[profile]; ok {
293			return append([]string(nil), ladder...)
294		}
295		return append([]string(nil), defaultProfileLadders["default"]...)
296	}

18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
19	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
20	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
21	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
22	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
23		Log          io.Writer
24		EventSink    io.Writer
25		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
26		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
27		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, is invoked between nextCandidate and
28		// Claim. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute against the
29		// loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed routing
30		// error the upstream surfaced — notably agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible
31		// when the harness allow-list rejects the model. Any non-nil error
32		// causes the loop to exit immediately without claiming the bead, with
33		// a worker-level execution_failed record naming the rejected pair.
34		// DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list; this gate only
35		// consumes the typed-incompatibility surface.
36		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
37		Once           bool
38		PollInterval   time.Duration
39		NoReview       bool
40		LabelFilter    string
41		SessionID      string
42		WorkerID       string
43		ProjectRoot    string
44	}

46	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
47	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
48	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
49	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
50	
51	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
52	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
53	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
54	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
55	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
56	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
57	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
58	
59	// capLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
60	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
61	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
62	func capLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
63		if d <= 0 {
64			return 0
65		}
66		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
67			return MaxLoopCooldown
68		}
69		return d
70	}

14	// TestLoadConfigHardErrorsOnDefaultHarness covers AC #1 of bead
15	// ddx-87fb72c2: a config carrying agent.routing.default_harness must
16	// fail to load with a migration message. The CLI surfaces the error to
17	// the user; the loader's exit code is non-zero (the *cobra.Command path
18	// returns the error to its caller).
19	func TestLoadConfigHardErrorsOnDefaultHarness(t *testing.T) {
20		tempDir := t.TempDir()
21		ddxDir := filepath.Join(tempDir, ".ddx")
22		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
23		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(`version: "1.0"
24	library:
25	  path: "./library"
26	  repository:
27	    url: "https://github.com/test/repo"
28	    branch: "main"
29	agent:
30	  routing:
31	    default_harness: claude
32	`), 0644))
33	
34		_, err := LoadWithWorkingDir(tempDir)
35		require.Error(t, err, "loading must fail when agent.routing.default_harness is present")
36	
37		migErr, ok := err.(*RoutingMigrationError)
38		require.True(t, ok, "expected RoutingMigrationError, got %T: %v", err, err)
39		assert.Equal(t, "agent.routing.default_harness", migErr.Field)
40	
41		msg := err.Error()
42		assert.Contains(t, msg, "agent.routing.default_harness")
43		assert.Contains(t, msg, "removed")
44		assert.Contains(t, msg, "docs/migrations/routing-config.md")
45	}
46	
47	// TestLoadConfigWarnsOnceForOptInRoutingFields covers AC #2: configs
48	// containing profile_ladders + model_overrides load successfully but
49	// emit a one-time process warning, AND the default execute path does
50	// not consult those fields. Consultation only happens when --escalate /
51	// --override-model are explicitly passed.
52	func TestLoadConfigWarnsOnceForOptInRoutingFields(t *testing.T) {
53		ResetRoutingDeprecationWarnings()
54		t.Cleanup(ResetRoutingDeprecationWarnings)
55	
56		tempDir := t.TempDir()
57		ddxDir := filepath.Join(tempDir, ".ddx")
58		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
59		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(`version: "1.0"
60	library:
61	  path: "./library"
62	  repository:
63	    url: "https://github.com/test/repo"
64	    branch: "main"
65	agent:
66	  routing:
67	    profile_ladders:
68	      default: [cheap, standard, smart]
69	    model_overrides:
70	      cheap: qwen/qwen3.6
71	`), 0644))
72	
73		oldStderr := os.Stderr
74		r, w, err := os.Pipe()
75		require.NoError(t, err)
76		os.Stderr = w
77		t.Cleanup(func() { os.Stderr = oldStderr })
78	
79		cfg, loadErr := LoadWithWorkingDir(tempDir)
80		require.NoError(t, loadErr)
81		require.NotNil(t, cfg)
82	
83		// Calling load a second time must NOT re-emit the same warning.
84		_, _ = LoadWithWorkingDir(tempDir)
85	
86		require.NoError(t, w.Close())
87		os.Stderr = oldStderr
88		out, err := io.ReadAll(r)
89		require.NoError(t, err)
90		stderrStr := string(out)
91	
92		assert.Contains(t, stderrStr, "agent.routing.profile_ladders is opt-in")
93		assert.Contains(t, stderrStr, "agent.routing.model_overrides is opt-in")
94		assert.Contains(t, stderrStr, "--escalate")
95		assert.Contains(t, stderrStr, "--override-model")
96		// Second load does not duplicate the warning lines.
97		assert.Equal(t, 1, strings.Count(stderrStr, "agent.routing.profile_ladders is opt-in"),
98			"profile_ladders warning must fire once per process")
99		assert.Equal(t, 1, strings.Count(stderrStr, "agent.routing.model_overrides is opt-in"),
100			"model_overrides warning must fire once per process")
101	
102		// Config still parses these fields so opt-in flags can consult them.
103		require.NotNil(t, cfg.Agent)
104		require.NotNil(t, cfg.Agent.Routing)
105		require.Contains(t, cfg.Agent.Routing.ProfileLadders, "default")
106		assert.Equal(t, "qwen/qwen3.6", cfg.Agent.Routing.ModelOverrides["cheap"])
107	}

51	// TestRoutingPreflightRejectionExitsLoopWithoutClaim covers ddx-98e6e9ef:
52	// when RoutePreflight returns the upstream typed-incompatibility error
53	// (agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible), the loop must NOT claim the bead,
54	// must NOT invoke the executor, must record a worker-level execution_failed
55	// result naming the rejected (harness, model) pair, and must exit.
56	func TestRoutingPreflightRejectionExitsLoopWithoutClaim(t *testing.T) {
57		inner, candidate, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
58		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
59	
60		var execCount int32
61		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
62			Store: store,
63			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
64				atomic.AddInt32(&execCount, 1)
65				return ExecuteBeadReport{BeadID: beadID, Status: ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess}, nil
66			}),
67		}
68	
69		// Fabricate a typed-incompatibility error via the upstream test seam:
70		// the preflight callback returns the same error type that upstream
71		// ResolveRoute returns when a harness allow-list rejects the model.
72		rejected := agentlib.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible{
73			Harness:         "claude",
74			Model:           "gpt-5",
75			SupportedModels: []string{"claude-opus-4-7", "claude-sonnet-4-6"},
76		}
77		var preflightCalls int32
78		preflight := func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error {
79			atomic.AddInt32(&preflightCalls, 1)
80			return rejected
81		}
82	
83		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{
84			Assignee: "worker",
85			Harness:  "claude",
86			Model:    "gpt-5",
87		}
88		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
89	
90		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
91			Once:           true,
92			RoutePreflight: preflight,
93		})
94		require.NoError(t, err)
95		require.NotNil(t, result)
96	
97		// AC#1: preflight ran before Claim.
98		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&preflightCalls), "preflight must be invoked")
99	
100		// AC#2: Claim was NEVER called on the store.
101		assert.Equal(t, int32(0), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls),
102			"beadStore.Claim must not be called when preflight rejects")
103	
104		// Executor must not have been invoked either.
105		assert.Equal(t, int32(0), atomic.LoadInt32(&execCount),
106			"executor must not run when preflight rejects")
107	
108		// AC#3: worker-level execution_failed record names the rejected pair.
109		require.Len(t, result.Results, 1, "exactly one worker-level failure record")
110		report := result.Results[0]
111		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, report.Status)
112		assert.Equal(t, candidate.ID, report.BeadID)
113		assert.Equal(t, "claude", report.Harness)
114		assert.Equal(t, "gpt-5", report.Model)
115		assert.Contains(t, report.Detail, "claude", "detail must name rejected harness")
116		assert.Contains(t, report.Detail, "gpt-5", "detail must name rejected model")
117		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, result.LastFailureStatus)
118		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Failures)
119	
120		// AC#4: bead has no kind:tier-attempt event from this attempt.
121		events, err := store.Events(candidate.ID)
122		require.NoError(t, err)
123		for _, ev := range events {
124			assert.NotEqual(t, "tier-attempt", ev.Kind,
125				"no tier-attempt event must be recorded when preflight rejects pre-claim")
126		}
127	
128		// Bead must remain open with no owner — confirms no Claim side-effects.
129		got, err := store.Get(candidate.ID)
130		require.NoError(t, err)
131		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status)
132		assert.Empty(t, got.Owner)
133	}

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(agent): route virtual/script harnesses through local runner in dispatchViaResolvedConfig; update bead-review golden [ddx-fdd3ea36]

dispatchViaResolvedConfig lacked the virtual/script harness short-circuit
that RunWithConfigViaService has. This caused TestAgentCompareDispatchUsesConfigResolve
and TestAgentQuorumDispatchUsesConfigResolve to fail when the compare/quorum
path sent "virtual" to the upstream service instead of DDx's record/replay runner.

The fix adds the same harness check as RunWithConfigViaService: when the
effective harness (HarnessOverride or rcfg.Harness()) is "virtual" or "script",
create a local Runner and dispatch through it. This makes DDX_VIRTUAL_RESPONSES
work correctly via the compare and quorum paths.

Also regenerates cli/cmd/testdata/bead_review/collapse_golden.txt to match
the current BuildReviewPrompt JSON schema output (was stale from a prior
review-format change).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

The commit is in place. All pre-commit hooks passed (large-files, secrets, conflicts, go-fmt, evidence-lint, runtime-lint, go-build, go-lint, go-test all green).

**Summary of what was done:**

The bead's routing changes (D1-D5) were already implemented in the codebase at the base commit. The execution found and fixed two pre-existing bugs that were blocking the test suite:

1. **`cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go`**: Added the `virtual`/`script` harness short-circuit that was present in `RunWithConfigViaService` but missing from `dispatchViaResolvedConfig`. This caused `TestAgentCompareDispatchUsesConfigResolve` and `TestAgentQuorumDispatchUsesConfigResolve` to fail because the compare/quorum paths sent "virtual" to the upstream service instead of DDx's local record/replay runner.

2. **`cli/cmd/testdata/bead_review/collapse_golden.txt`**: Regenerated the stale golden file to match the current JSON-schema-based `BuildReviewPrompt` output format.

All bead ACs were already satisfied in the codebase — the `--escalate` flag, preflight gate, config deprecation hard errors/warnings, default-path `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")`, and regression tests are all present and passing.
